title: A Quiet Place: Day One Box Office Opening
text: A Quiet Place: Day One achieved a franchise record opening weekend, earning $53 million domestically across its first three days, surpassing the previous installments' performances. The prequel opened in 3,707 theaters and pulled in $22.5 million on Friday, followed by $17 million Saturday and $13.5 million Sunday. The film's global debut reached $98.5 million. According to box office data, the film drew 3.6 million patrons domestically. Paramount noted that 66% of ticket sales came from same-day purchases, indicating strong walk-up business driven by a coast-to-coast heat wave. Premium formats performed exceptionally well, accounting for 39% of the weekend's total take, with IMAX contributing $5.8 million from 408 North American locations. The film attracted a diverse audience: 39% Caucasian, 32% Hispanic/Latino, 16% Black, and 9% Asian viewers. The 18-34 age group represented 55% of attendees, while women over 25 gave the highest approval ratings at 81% on PostTrak. The film earned a B+ CinemaScore and received an 86% Certified Fresh rating on Rotten Tomatoes, though these marked the franchise's lowest critical scores to date.
